timeline.cause: The container is being OOM-killed: the kernel terminates it (exit 137) every time it crosses its 256Mi memory limit, and kubelet restarts it into the same wall — that's the crash loop. The limit stopped fitting the workload; the logs show memory climbing right up to the kill.
receipts: 14:19:26 INFO  warming promo bundle cache: 8/24 shards, rss=141MiB

timeline.explain: Double the memory limit (256Mi → 512Mi) so the service stops getting killed mid-request. This buys stability now; it does not fix why memory grew.
timeline.followup: Open a ticket on the memory growth itself — if it's a leak, the new limit only reschedules this page.
